opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Whenever I walk up the front steps, my chest feels like it's in a vice, and the pain shoots straight up into my jaw.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: New episodes of exertional chest tightness over the last two days, radiating to the jaw and arm, brought on by minimal exertion.

symptom_narrative.duration: episodes lasting a few minutes, brought on by exertion and fully settling with 5-10 minutes of rest
symptom_narrative.character: a vice-like tightness in the centre of the chest
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: severe enough that he now avoids exertion for fear of bringing it on
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: central/retrosternal chest, shooting up into the jaw and into the left arm
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: comes on predictably with exertion (walking up stairs, carrying laundry), settles with rest; happening more easily over the last two days
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting down and resting relieves it within 5-10 minutes
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: intermittent nausea and mild breathlessness with the episodes; currently pain-free at rest
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: never had this before - it started two days ago and is happening more easily
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: modifying his behaviour to avoid exertion because he is frightened of the pain

past_medical_history.how_managed: on a daily cholesterol tablet but admits he often misses doses

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: frightened by the new chest pain but lives alone and worried about coping
